text: Gen. Caine confirms 5,000-lb penetrators used on underground storage sites. 120+ Iranian naval vessels sunk. A-10s actively hunting boats in Hormuz. Iran's surface fleet 'no longer a factor.' 11 submarines eliminated.
verificationNote: Pentagon briefing confirmed by CBS, CNBC

text: UNSC Resolution 2817 passes 13-0-2 — first UN condemnation of Iran's attacks on Gulf neighbors. Russia and China abstain. France calls Iran 'primarily responsible for escalation.' Major diplomatic milestone.
verificationNote: UN Press Release SC16315

text: Brent crude briefly hits $119/barrel before settling at $108.65 after Netanyahu says Israel will help reopen Hormuz. Analysts at Wood Mackenzie warn $150-$200/barrel 'not outside the realm of possibility.' Goldman Sachs: 16.1M bpd effectively cut.
verificationNote: CNBC, Yahoo Finance confirmed market data

text: IDF confirmed first-ever strikes on Bandar Anzali, Iran's Caspian Sea port. Five missile boats destroyed along with a naval headquarters and shipyard. Represents deepest geographic reach of the air campaign so far.
verificationNote: IDF spokesperson confirmation; Alma Research Center daily report

text: Pentagon briefing reveals US has struck 7,800+ targets in Iran. All 11 subs and 120+ warships destroyed. Hegseth announced 'largest strike package yet' coming today — $200B supplemental requested from Congress.
verificationNote: Pentagon briefing aired live, CBS News confirmed

text: IMO announces negotiations for humanitarian corridor to free ~20,000 seafarers stranded in Gulf by Hormuz blockade. 7-nation Hormuz coalition (UK, FR, DE, IT, NL, JP, CA) issues joint statement calling for safe passage and infrastructure moratorium.
verificationNote: GOV.UK joint statement published; CNN confirmed IMO move

text: IRGC confirms spokesman Gen. Naeini killed in overnight US-Israeli strike on Tehran/Karaj. 4th senior Iranian leader killed this week. IRGC Aerospace Force at 75% launcher degradation.
verificationNote: IRGC official confirmation

text: Israel launching Nowruz strikes on Tehran — multiple explosions. IDF: 'wave of strikes targeting Iranian terror regime.' Brent spiked to $119.11 before settling at $110.71.
verificationNote: Haaretz, CNN confirmed

text: Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi (730K bpd) hit by Iranian drones for 2nd straight day. Saudi SAMREF at Yanbu struck. Dubai air defenses intercepting Iranian fire on Eid.
verificationNote: US News, Argus Media confirmed

text: Pentagon $200B supplemental to Congress. First 100h cost $3.7B, 12 days $16.5B (CSIS). USS Boxer ARG with 11th MEU departed SD — 2nd MEU to theater.
verificationNote: WaPo, JPost confirmed

text: Hormuz Day 18: Iran selective transits to friendly nations. 8 non-Iranian vessels Mar 18. IEA: largest supply disruption in history — 8 mb/d plunge. 400+ tankers anchored.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, CNBC confirmed

text: DNI Gabbard: Iran NOT rebuilding enrichment before war — undercuts justification. Kent resigned NCTC. Araghchi rejected ceasefire. Nuclear material 'under the rubbles.'
verificationNote: FP, WaPo, Time confirmed

text: Mojtaba Khamenei Nowruz message via TV anchor/still photo — NOT appeared since becoming Supreme Leader. US claims wounded/disfigured. Reports of Moscow evacuation for surgery.
verificationNote: Statement confirmed; injury unverified

text: 100M+ views on AI war deepfakes (BBC Verify). NYT: 110+ unique pro-Iran deepfakes. Russia amplifying. X Grok misidentifying fakes. FDD: coordinated Iranian AI disinfo campaign.
verificationNote: CNN, FDD confirmed

text: Switzerland formally halted new arms export licences to the US citing neutrality law — becoming the first NATO partner country to invoke neutrality against the US in an ongoing conflict. Swiss law requires this when they assess a conflict constitutes a formal war.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, Al Jazeera and Times of Israel confirmed Swiss Federal Council announcement

text: Trump's Pearl Harbor quip to Japanese PM Takaichi — comparing Iran surprise attack to the 1941 bombing — is drawing sharp diplomatic alarm in Tokyo. Takaichi remained composed but Japan has not publicly committed to Hormuz patrol role. NATO rejection earlier this week repeated pattern.
verificationNote: CNBC, ABC News, Euronews confirmed the Oval Office remark and Japan's cautious response

text: IRGC Spokesman Gen. Naeini killed in Israeli airstrike hours after publicly insisting Iran was 'still building missiles' — a direct rebuttal of Netanyahu's claim Iran cannot make ballistic missiles. Now the 5th senior Iranian military/security official killed this week.
verificationNote: AP, NPR, Fox News confirmed killing; India TV News confirmed IRGC statement

text: Mina al-Ahmadi refinery (Kuwait, ~730,000 bpd capacity) struck again by Iranian drones on Day 21. Multiple units on fire early morning. UAE air defenses engaged 4 ballistic missiles and 26 UAVs overnight. UAE total since Feb 28: 338 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,740 UAVs intercepted.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Fox News, UAE Defense Ministry confirmed; AP confirmed refinery fire

text: UN IMO moves to establish humanitarian corridor to free ~20,000 seafarers stranded in the Gulf. UK sent military planners to work with US on 'viable collective plan' to reopen Hormuz. No timeframe — Iran's cooperation unclear. Oil at $110/barrel as Nowruz strikes add to energy volatility.
verificationNote: CNN Day 21 coverage confirmed IMO announcement and UK planners

text: Israel struck Tehran on Nowruz (Persian New Year) and Eid al-Fitr, coinciding with both Iranian and Muslim celebrations. Airstrikes audible across capital as Iranians described a 'very different' Nowruz this year — marking the new year amid power cuts, displacement and ongoing bombardment.
verificationNote: CNN Nowruz feature, NPR, Fox News confirmed morning strikes on Tehran

text: Guterres issued his strongest statement yet at EU Council in Brussels — directly addressed US and Israel to 'end this war' and told Iran to 'stop attacking your neighbors.' First time Guterres named all three parties explicitly. Swiss simultaneously invoked neutrality. Signs of widening diplomatic break with Washington.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera video of Guterres Brussels statement; UN News confirmed

text: OSINT analysts tracking Israel's Nowruz-morning strikes on Tehran report multiple large explosions across eastern districts of the capital. IDF confirmed targeting 'infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran' — Al Jazeera Tehran correspondent describes capital as 'hushed' with no celebrations visible.
verificationNote: IDF announced; Al Jazeera correspondent on-ground confirmation

text: Multiple credible Iranian sources reporting Brigadier General Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesman) killed in Nowruz dawn strikes. IRGC subsequently confirmed his death — marking the 4th senior Iranian official eliminated this week. Naini had just publicly claimed Iran's missile industry score was '20 out of 20' hours before being killed.
verificationNote: IRGC officially confirmed; Al Jazeera, ToI, NPR all corroborated

text: TankerTrackers reports AIS data shows zero tanker transits through Hormuz in the past 24 hours — Day 18 of effective blockade. Approximately 400+ vessels remain anchored in wait positions. Bessent's announcement of possible 140M barrel Iranian crude unsanctioning is being watched closely by tanker operators.
verificationNote: AIS satellite data consistent with blockade pattern

text: Aurora Intel analysis: Mojtaba Khamenei's first public statement as Supreme Leader ('Iran's enemies must have their security taken away') signals continuity of Iran's war posture despite massive leadership losses. His absence from public view for 21 days was likely security-driven — Iran International notes he has not been photographed since assuming power.
verificationNote: Statement attributed via NPR and Al Jazeera coverage

text: Sentdefender tracking Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery fire — second Iranian drone strike on this facility in 24 hours. The 730,000 bpd complex is Kuwait's primary refinery; repeated targeting may force a shutdown. Kuwaiti authorities confirmed summoning Iran's ambassador.
verificationNote: Kuwaiti government confirmed the drone strike and fire

text: Reuters reports Brent crude rose 1.3% to $110.28/barrel on Day 21 as Treasury Secretary Bessent confirmed the US may lift sanctions on approximately 140 million barrels of Iranian crude currently on tankers at sea. Saudi officials separately warned oil could hit $180/barrel if disruptions persist through late April.
verificationNote: CNBC, Reuters, Bloomberg all confirmed Bessent statement and oil prices

text: War Monitor tracking IDF claims of 70% of Iran's ~500 ballistic missile launchers destroyed or disabled as of Day 21. If accurate, Iran's remaining capacity is now estimated at 150 operational launchers — still sufficient for daily salvos but drastically reduced from pre-war levels. IDF simultaneously claims air supremacy over most Iranian airspace after neutralizing 100+ air defense systems.
verificationNote: IDF military claim; independently unverifiable due to Iran internet blackout and classified assessment methods

text: Intel Crab reports missile fragments confirmed landing in Jerusalem's Jewish Quarter on Day 21 — approximately 400 meters from the Western Wall. This is the closest Iranian ordnance has landed to Judaism's holiest accessible site since the war began. No casualties reported; incident escalates symbolic stakes dramatically as strikes coincide with Nowruz and Eid al-Fitr.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog and multiple Israeli sources confirmed fragments in Old City Jewish Quarter

text: Sentdefender analysis: Trump's 'COWARDS' Truth Social post targeting NATO represents the sharpest US attack on the alliance since the war began. Six allies (UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan) offered post-ceasefire Hormuz support — conditions Trump explicitly rejected. The rift signals the US may seek bilateral arrangements to open Hormuz rather than operating under NATO framework.
verificationNote: Trump Truth Social post confirmed by Bloomberg, CNN, Al Jazeera; six-nation joint statement confirmed

text: Aurora Intel: IDF kills Hezbollah intelligence chief Hussein Makled in Beirut overnight strikes — a significant degradation of Hezbollah's command and control. This follows elimination of Hezbollah's Al Manar TV director earlier this week. Lebanon death toll now exceeds 1,001 with 1,049,328 displaced, nearly 1 in 5 Lebanese uprooted. New Israeli evacuation orders suggest expanded ground operations south of Litani River imminent.
verificationNote: IDF confirmed Makled killing; Anadolu Agency and Al Jazeera reported evacuation orders

text: Reuters reports US deploying additional 2,500 Marines and USS Boxer Amphibious Ready Group to Middle East, bringing total US troop presence to ~50,000. The deployment is linked to potential Strait of Hormuz operations. Trump publicly denied sending more troops while Pentagon officials confirmed the deployment — the contradiction highlighted tensions between Trump's messaging and military reality.
verificationNote: Stars and Stripes, Military.com, NPR confirmed the deployment
